title: Why are the NERV staff in episode 26
display_title: Why are the NERV staff in episode 26
wikitext:
[]: Why are the NERV staff in episode 26?             Quote Post by chaosakita » Fri Jan 01, 2010 9:49 pm In the TV ending, we have pretty much all of the living characters we've seen in Evangelion. So there's Rei, Asuka, Misato, his schoolmates, his parents...and the NERV staff too! There's a lot of different interpretations of the scene, but it appears that Shinji's friends are trying to help him overcome his self doubts and stuff. The problem is, the NERV staff aren't really Shinji's friends. Despite their many appearances in the show, how many times has the main character really met them?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Jan 01, 2010 9:53 pm The bridge bunnies are there because, like everyone else, they are apart of instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Fri Jan 01, 2010 9:56 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: The bridge bunnies are there because, like everyone else, they are apart of instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Jan 01, 2010 10:03 pm There's some evidence that they were fairly familiar or at least friendly with Shinji (is it Hyuuga who tries to talk him down after the dummyplug incident?), which makes some sense since they probably interact with the pilots more than anyone except Misato and Ritsuko. Also, they're recurring speaking characters who Shinji knew the names of, which pretty much automatically qualifies them for a place in Shinji's Instrumentality experience. Frankly, though, I never really liked their inclusion in 26 either. It's all very well to have Asuka, Misato, Kaji, etc. giving Shinji postlife advice, but it seems a little odd when the bunnies do it. I much prefer their role in 26', which is to play straight man while the world's going to hell. Instrumentality wouldn't have the impact it does (no pun intended) if not for the fact that we've seen just enough of the bunnies up to this point that we sort of care about them, but not enough to cry when they get tanged. The fact that we see their tanging and transition guides is a brilliant little stroke by Anno and a wonderful insight into their characters of the sort that you don't often see. I only wish we'd gotten the same courtesy extended to Touji, Hikari and Kensuke. I'm always rather disappointed that they get tanged offscreen. Although, come to think of it, the explosion from the Series' AT fields may have just blown them up the old-fashioned way, depending on how far from Tokyo-3 they moved. /offtopic

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Fri Jan 01, 2010 10:06 pm I can easily explain Maya's presence with Two Words SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Fri Jan 01, 2010 10:18 pm Shinji presumably sees the techies at work every day, and seems to know their names. Also, the bridge trio is in the keepsake photo in EoE, which iirc the script says was actually taken in "the real world." Maybe he talked to Hyuga at a company picnic or something. :P

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Jan 01, 2010 10:48 pm Well, the rest of the world isn't, right? (Like the Jet Alone guy) Are the people shown there just supposed to be a representation of everyone in the world?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jan 02, 2010 12:36 am Because Shinji knows them.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sat Jan 02, 2010 1:33 am GasmaskAvenger wrote: I can easily explain Maya's presence with Two Words SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Sat Jan 02, 2010 2:03 am esselfortium wrote: Hey broseph, quit making dumb references to character names from your RP in unrelated threads! Thants!

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Jan 02, 2010 12:45 pm These are the people Shinji knew in life. Those who now try to persuade him to come back to it rather than "run away", i.e. commit suicide.

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Sat Jan 02, 2010 1:24 pm What about his classmates though? His teacher perhaps? He knows these people too. They're just the people the viewer knows well enough, and the seiyus had to earn their paychecks.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jan 02, 2010 2:04 pm The three classmates he interacted with are there. I don't think Shinji is social enough to really notice more than that.

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Sat Jan 02, 2010 2:54 pm AuraTwilight wrote: The three classmates he interacted with are there. I don't think Shinji is social enough to really notice more than that.

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Sun Jan 03, 2010 11:07 pm well pen pen didnt show up, and he was in the picture too. and i also believe the classmates were killed during zeruel attack.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jan 03, 2010 11:34 pm bladerj wrote: and i also believe the classmates were killed during zeruel attack.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jan 03, 2010 11:34 pm Pen-Pen is shown in different parts of both endings.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Jan 04, 2010 3:52 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Pen-Pen is shown in different parts of both endings.

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Mon Jan 04, 2010 4:06 pm I don't think it's a real matter if Shinji personally knew them or not, hell I don't think he even talked to Aoba during any point in the show minus 26. It's more like instrumentality had taken effect and all the souls that were at least partially known to Shinji had begun to talk with him. The people more close to Shinji (Asuka, Misato and Rei) get more screen time, obviously.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Mon Jan 04, 2010 4:39 pm The thing I always found strange about Instrumentality was that individuals were still represented separately. I suppose that means that the final step was not shown in the tv ending. EoE fixed this thankfully. At least in Eotv, the rest of humanity just didnt care about Shinji to help him with his problems. Hell, there were probably thousands more people with far worse psychological problems getting ready to absorbed, being counseled by their friends, as well as others like Shinji that were still separate from the humanity without loved ones telling them to bring down their walls
